data.title: Assessing Argan (AGX) Valuation After Insider Sale Dividend Hike And Earnings Beat
data.description:
Argan (AGX) is back on investor watchlists after Director Peter W Getsinger sold roughly US$3.38 million in shares, shortly after the company lifted its quarterly cash dividend by 33% and reported earnings ahead of expectations.

See our latest analysis for Argan.

Those events come after a sharp run, with Argan’s 1 year total shareholder return close to 100% and multi year total shareholder returns several times higher, even as recent share price returns have softened and short term momentum has cooled.

If Argan’s recent moves have caught your eye, it could be a good moment to see what else is out there and scan fast growing stocks with high insider ownership.

With Argan’s shares up roughly 100% over the past year, recent returns softening, a higher dividend and an insider sale all on the table, you have to ask: is there still value here or is the market already pricing in future growth?

Most Popular Narrative: 14.3% Undervalued

With Argan last closing at US$309.26 versus a narrative fair value of US$361.00, the story centers on how future projects could support that gap.

Record backlog and continued project wins across gas, renewables, water treatment, and recycling plants provide multi-year revenue visibility, indicating potential for increased operating leverage and higher gross margins as larger projects are executed successfully.

Read the complete narrative.

Curious what earnings path is built into that price gap? Revenue growth, margin shifts, and a richer future P/E all sit at the core of this narrative. The full set of projections spells out how today’s construction pipeline could translate into tomorrow’s profits.

However, the picture is not one way; heavy exposure to large gas projects and any slip in execution or permitting could quickly challenge the current growth story.

The narrative fair value points to upside, but the current P/E of 35.8x tells a different story. It sits above both peers at 34.1x and the fair ratio of 31.3x, which suggests the market is already paying up and leaves less room if expectations cool.

data.title: Does Expanded Rapiblyk Distribution Hint at a Deeper Shift in Cardinal Health’s (CAH) Specialty Strategy?
data.description:
• AOP Health US, LLC recently expanded US access to its critical care drug Rapiblyk (landiolol) through Cardinal Health and other major distributors, allowing hospitals and health systems to source it via their usual channels.
  • This broadened distribution highlights Cardinal Health’s role as a core conduit for complex therapies in acute care settings, reinforcing its importance in the specialty pharmaceutical supply chain.
  • We’ll now examine how this wider Rapiblyk distribution through Cardinal Health could influence the company’s investment narrative and long-term positioning.

Cardinal Health Investment Narrative Recap

To own Cardinal Health, you need to believe in the durability of its role at the center of the pharmaceutical and medical supply chain, with modest earnings growth and tight margins. The expanded Rapiblyk distribution supports this core distributor story but does not materially change the near term picture, where regulatory and reimbursement uncertainty remain key risks and upcoming earnings updates are still the main catalyst to watch.

The most relevant recent announcement here is AOP Health US widening Rapiblyk access through Cardinal Health and other major distributors, underscoring Cardinal’s position in complex, acute care therapies. This sits alongside progress in its medical distribution business under new management, which many investors are watching closely as a potential earnings and margin catalyst if Cardinal can keep improving service levels and profitability.

Yet while Cardinal’s role in critical care drugs is growing, investors still need to consider the risk that tighter government pricing and reimbursement pressure could...

data.title: Assessing Ferrari (BIT:RACE) After Completing Its €2b Share Buyback Program
data.description:
Ferrari (BIT:RACE) has completed its multiyear share buyback, repurchasing 6,015,933 shares, or 3.34% of its stock, for €2,000 million, including 717,508 shares between October and late December 2025.

See our latest analysis for Ferrari.

Ferrari’s €2,000 million buyback comes after a softer period for the shares, with a 1-year total shareholder return decline of 23.69% and a stronger 5-year total shareholder return of 85.87%. Recent 90-day share price returns show a 7.00% decline, suggesting momentum has cooled.

If this kind of capital return story has your attention, it could be a good moment to compare Ferrari with other auto manufacturers that might be lining up their next move.

With Ferrari buying back over 3% of its stock after a 23.69% 1-year total return decline, the key question for you is simple: is this a genuine value opportunity, or is the market already pricing in future growth?

Advertisement

Most Popular Narrative: 23.1% Undervalued

With Ferrari shares last closing at €311.0, the most followed narrative sees fair value materially higher, based on detailed growth and profitability assumptions.

Ongoing investments in innovation (e.g., electrification, new manufacturing/paint facilities, and cross-sector technology transfers like the Hypersail project) both future-proof the business and leverage secular trends towards luxury experiential goods, likely resulting in higher capital efficiency and supporting sustainable earnings growth over the next cycle.

Read the complete narrative.

Curious what kind of revenue mix, margin profile, and earnings path could justify that higher value and rich future P/E multiple? The narrative lays out a very specific growth runway, tight share count assumptions and a required return hurdle that might surprise you.

Result: Fair Value of €404.24 (UNDERVALUED)

Have a read of the narrative in full and understand what's behind the forecasts.

However, this story can shift quickly if the electric and hybrid rollout stumbles, or if changing luxury tastes start to chip away at Ferrari’s pricing power.

Find out about the key risks to this Ferrari narrative.

Another View: High Quality, High Price

That 23.1% undervaluation story faces a clear challenge once you look at what the market is already paying for Ferrari. On a P/E of 35.8x, the shares trade at roughly double the global auto industry average of 18x and roughly double the peer average of 17.4x.

Our fair ratio for Ferrari sits at 19.6x, well below the current 35.8x. In plain terms, the market is pricing Ferrari as a premium business with an outstanding 42.4% return on equity and high quality earnings, but also reflecting valuation risk if sentiment or growth expectations cool. Which narrative do you find more convincing?

data.title: Did Barclays’ Upgrade and EQT’s Exit Just Shift Kodiak Gas Services' (KGS) Investment Narrative?
data.description:
• In recent days, Barclays upgraded Kodiak Gas Services to an Overweight rating, while the company announced a dual listing on NYSE Texas and the full exit of EQT Infrastructure’s remaining stake via an underwritten public offering.
  • These moves not only underscore sustained demand for Kodiak’s compression services tied to natural gas volume growth, but also mark a meaningful shift in its shareholder base and governance influence.
  • We’ll now examine how Barclays’ more positive stance, alongside EQT Infrastructure’s exit, may influence Kodiak Gas Services’ existing investment narrative.

Trump's oil boom is here - pipelines are primed to profit. Discover the 22 US stocks riding the wave.

Advertisement

Kodiak Gas Services Investment Narrative Recap

To own Kodiak Gas Services, you need to be comfortable with a capital intensive compression business that depends on healthy natural gas volumes and solid fleet utilization. Barclays’ upgrade and the dual listing do not materially change the near term demand catalyst, but EQT Infrastructure’s full exit sharpens attention on execution risks such as labor availability in the Permian and the company’s ability to sustain margins in a concentrated, large horsepower segment.

The most relevant recent move here is EQT Infrastructure’s underwritten public offering of its remaining stake, which also ends its board nomination rights. This shift in Kodiak’s shareholder base and governance framework comes as investors weigh ongoing compression demand against risks like potential overcapacity and pricing pressure if natural gas growth slows or the segment becomes more commoditized.

However, investors should also be aware that Kodiak’s focus on large horsepower compression could leave it more exposed if...

Kodiak Gas Services' narrative projects $1.5 billion revenue and $293.4 million earnings by 2028. This requires 5.8% yearly revenue growth and about a $210 million earnings increase from $83.2 million today.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Three members of the Simply Wall St Community see fair value for Kodiak Gas Services between US$44.20 and US$115.67, highlighting very different expectations. data.title: How Edenred’s Oversubscribed €500 Million Bond Issue At Edenred (ENXTPA:EDEN) Has Changed Its Investment Story
data.description:
• Edenred recently issued a €500 million bond maturing in January 2033 with a 3.75% coupon, attracting demand of over €1.60 billion from international institutional investors and using the proceeds mainly to refinance a bond due in March 2026.
  • This oversubscribed issue extends Edenred’s average bond maturity to 4.1 years and underpins funding for its Amplify25-28 growth plan.
  • Next, we’ll examine how this strongly oversubscribed €500 million bond issue may influence Edenred’s investment narrative and perceived risk profile.

Edenred Investment Narrative Recap

To own Edenred, you need to believe in its ability to compound fee-based digital payment revenues while managing regulatory, FX and competitive pressures. The oversubscribed €500 million bond should not materially change the key near term catalyst, which is execution of the Amplify25-28 plan, or the main current risk around slower growth and margin pressure in mature European markets.

The recent alliance with Visa in Europe looks particularly relevant here, as the new bond improves Edenred’s capacity to keep investing in its issuing and processing infrastructure. Together, they support the push into higher value B2B payments and mobility solutions, which many investors see as central to Edenred’s medium term growth potential.

Yet, against this backdrop, the risk that heavier technology and regulatory spending could compress margins is something investors should be aware of...

data.title: Assessing Axcelis Technologies (ACLS) Valuation After Recent Share Price Momentum And Mixed Earnings Outlook
data.description:
Advertisement

Axcelis Technologies overview and recent performance context

Axcelis Technologies (ACLS) has attracted attention after a period of mixed financial trends, with annual revenue growth of 2.99% and a 13.63% decline in net income, prompting investors to reassess the current share price of $95.72.

See our latest analysis for Axcelis Technologies.

Recent trading has been positive, with a 1 month share price return of 11.81% and a 90 day share price return of 14.27%. The 1 year total shareholder return of 37.41% sits against a slightly negative 3 year total shareholder return and a strong 5 year record of 145.50%. This suggests momentum has picked up again after a mixed medium term.

If Axcelis has you looking closer at chip makers, this could be a good moment to scan other semiconductors and related names through high growth tech and AI stocks.

With Axcelis trading at $95.72, close to its analyst price target of $93.50 and with an intrinsic discount figure that is slightly negative, investors may question whether there is meaningful upside remaining or whether the market is already fully reflecting expectations for future growth.

Most Popular Narrative: 2% Overvalued

At a last close of $95.72 versus a narrative fair value of $93.50, the story here hinges on how future margins and earnings are expected to reset.

Analysts expect earnings to reach $66.7 million (and earnings per share of $4.29) by about August 2028, down from $158.5 million today. The analysts are largely in agreement about this estimate.

Read the complete narrative.

Curious why a lower earnings path still lines up with a higher future earnings multiple and a positive price target gap? The core assumptions sit in the revenue glide path, margin reset, and the premium P/E investors are expected to accept. The tension between shrinking profits and a richer valuation multiple is what shapes this fair value number.

Result: Fair Value of $93.50 (OVERVALUED)

Have a read of the narrative in full and understand what's behind the forecasts.

However, heavy China exposure and reliance on mature node demand mean that any shift in export controls or customer spending could quickly challenge this optimistic setup.

Find out about the key risks to this Axcelis Technologies narrative.

Another View: Earnings Multiple Sends a Different Signal

While the fair value narrative suggests Axcelis Technologies is about 2% overvalued, the current P/E of 21.6x tells a different story. It is far below the US Semiconductor industry average of 42x and the peer average of 56.1x, yet sits above the fair ratio of 15.7x.

In practical terms, that mix points to a share price that is cheaper than many direct comparisons but richer than what our fair ratio suggests the market could eventually lean toward. Is this a reasonable premium for Axcelis's position in ion implantation, or a valuation risk you want to be paid more for taking?

data.title: Assessing QuantumScape (QS) Valuation After Cobra Manufacturing Upgrade And Efficiency Gains
data.description:
Advertisement

QuantumScape’s manufacturing upgrade and why it matters for investors

QuantumScape (QS) reported that its Cobra ceramic separator process is now the standard in its solid-state lithium-metal battery manufacturing, improving production efficiency, supporting potential gigafactory-scale output, and targeting longer battery life, faster charging, and lower costs.

See our latest analysis for QuantumScape.

Despite excitement around the Cobra process, QuantumScape’s recent trading has been choppy, with a 30 day share price return of a 9.15% decline and a 90 day share price return of a 39.94% decline, even as the 1 year total shareholder return sits at 104.19%. This suggests earlier momentum has faded, but longer term holders have still seen strong gains overall.

If advances like QuantumScape’s Cobra process have you thinking about where battery and chip technologies could go next, this is a good time to look at high growth tech and AI stocks for more potential ideas in related areas.

With QuantumScape still loss making, trading below its average analyst price target and showing a very large intrinsic value gap, is the recent pullback a chance to buy into future growth or is the market already pricing it in?

QuantumScape (QS), a company that for years was often dismissed as a mere "science project" by skeptics, has definitively transformed into a formidable business on the verge of real-world impact1. Having chosen the "Hard Path" to tackle fundamental battery challenges, QuantumScape''s journey, spanning over a decade and fueled by approximately $1.5 billion in funding (with roughly $300 million strategically deployed to date), is now described as being poised for commercial success3.

Increasing clinical evidence, integration of AI/digital pathology, partnerships (Helix, biopharma), and a growing GRID/data platform are enhancing Veracyte's technology differentiation and providing leverage to value-based healthcare models. data.title: A Look At Brown & Brown (BRO) Valuation As Analyst Downgrades Highlight Softer Insurance Market
data.description:
Recent analyst actions around Brown & Brown (BRO) have turned attention to how a softening property and casualty insurance market could influence the company’s organic growth expectations, margins, and investor sentiment over the next few years.

See our latest analysis for Brown & Brown.

Brown & Brown’s recent analyst downgrades and concerns about softer property and casualty pricing sit against a share price of US$79.61, with a 90 day share price return of a 16% decline and a 1 year total shareholder return of a 21.87% loss. The 5 year total shareholder return of 77.83% suggests longer term holders have still seen substantial gains, so momentum appears weaker in the near term compared with the longer history.

If you are weighing up what to do next after this softer patch, it could be a good time to see how other insurers are positioned through healthcare stocks.

With the share price under pressure, revenue and net income growth still positive, and analysts trimming expectations, the key question is simple: is Brown & Brown now undervalued, or is the market already factoring in its future growth?

Advertisement

Most Popular Narrative: 13.7% Undervalued

Compared with Brown & Brown’s last close of US$79.61, the most followed narrative points to a fair value in the low US$90s. This frames current weakness as a potential valuation gap rather than a permanent reset.

Strong cash flow from operations, combined with a strategic focus on leveraging a diversified portfolio across geographies and lines of business, positions the company to navigate economic uncertainties and potentially increase future earnings stability and growth.

Read the complete narrative.

Curious what has to happen for that higher value to stack up? This narrative leans heavily on faster top line growth, resilient margins and a rich future earnings multiple. Want to see how those ingredients fit together and what discount rate is used to justify them? Read on to see the full playbook behind this fair value.

The most popular narrative applies a discount rate of 6.96% and projects Brown & Brown’s future cash generation out over several years, then brings those cash flows back to today’s terms to arrive at a fair value near US$92 per share.

Those assumptions sit alongside expectations for healthy revenue growth, slightly lower profit margins over time and a future P/E that remains above the broader US insurance peer group. Together, these factors help explain why the narrative arrives at a fair value above the current share price.

Result: Fair Value of $92.29 (UNDERVALUED)

Have a read of the narrative in full and understand what's behind the forecasts.

However, this depends on economic conditions and regulation cooperating. Slower revenue growth or unfavorable legislative changes could both quickly challenge the optimistic fair value case.

Find out about the key risks to this Brown & Brown narrative.

Another View: Richer Valuation On Earnings

The 13.7% DCF style undervaluation appears inconsistent with the earnings picture. Brown & Brown trades on a P/E of 27.5x, roughly double the US Insurance industry at 12.9x and well above its own fair ratio of 16.4x, which indicates potential valuation risk if sentiment cools.

See what the numbers say about this price — find out in our valuation breakdown.

data.title: Is Ulta Beauty (ULTA) Pricing In Too Much Good News After Strong Share Price Run
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether Ulta Beauty's share price still offers value after a strong run, this article will walk through what the current market price might imply about the stock.
  • Ulta Beauty recently closed at US$661.49, with returns of 1.8% over 7 days, 10.0% over 30 days, 6.7% year to date, 63.7% over 1 year, 33.7% over 3 years, and 125.6% over 5 years. These figures naturally raise questions about what is already priced in and how much risk you are taking on at these levels.
  • Recent coverage has continued to focus on Ulta Beauty's position in the US beauty retail market and how investors are thinking about its long term growth potential and competitive strengths. That backdrop is important context when assessing whether the recent share price performance lines up with what the fundamentals might support.
  • Simply Wall St currently gives Ulta Beauty a value score of 1 out of 6. We will break this down using several valuation approaches before coming back to a broader, and arguably more useful, way to think about what the stock is really worth.

Ulta Beauty scores just 1/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Ulta Beauty Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a business might be worth today by projecting future cash flows and then discounting those back to a single present value figure.

For Ulta Beauty, the model uses a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach. The latest twelve month free cash flow is about $952.2 million, and analysts provide explicit projections out to 2027, with an estimated free cash flow of $1,011.2 million in that year. Beyond that, Simply Wall St extrapolates out to 2035, with annual free cash flows in the model remaining below $1.3b and discounted back to today.

Pulling those projections together, the DCF model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of about $379.67 per share. Compared with the recent share price of $661.49, the model implies the stock is around 74.2% above this estimate, which points to a rich valuation on these cash flow assumptions.

Result: OVERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Ulta Beauty may be overvalued by 74.2%. Discover 883 undervalued stocks or create your own screener to find better value opportunities.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Ulta Beauty.

Approach 2: Ulta Beauty Price vs Earnings

For a profitable company like Ulta Beauty, the P/E ratio is a useful way to see how much you are paying for each dollar of current earnings. This makes it a straightforward check against the more detailed DCF work.

What counts as a "normal" P/E really depends on what the market expects for earnings growth and how risky those earnings are. Faster and more predictable growth can justify a higher P/E, while slower or less certain growth usually lines up with a lower P/E.

Ulta Beauty currently trades on a P/E of 24.66x. That sits above the Specialty Retail industry average P/E of 20.85x and below a peer group average of 37.01x, so the market is putting a higher multiple on Ulta Beauty than on the industry as a whole, but not as high as some peers.

Simply Wall St's Fair Ratio for Ulta Beauty is 17.46x. This is a proprietary estimate of what P/E might make sense given the company’s earnings growth profile, industry, profit margins, market cap and risk characteristics. It aims to be more tailored than a simple comparison with peers or the industry average, which do not fully capture those differences.

Comparing Ulta Beauty's actual P/E of 24.66x to the Fair Ratio of 17.46x suggests the shares are pricing in more optimism than this framework would support.

Result: OVERVALUED

P/E ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Discover 1443 companies where insiders are betting big on explosive growth.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your Ulta Beauty Narrative

Earlier we mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation, so let us introduce you to Narratives, which are simply your story about a company, linked directly to your assumptions for future revenue, earnings, margins and a fair value that you can compare to today’s price.

On Simply Wall St’s Community page, Narratives let you set out why you think Ulta Beauty might justify, for example, a fair value close to US$680 if you focus on wellness expansion, digital investments, loyalty strength and new markets, or closer to US$405 if you are more focused on rising costs, competition and the Target partnership ending. The platform then turns those views into a forecast and a fair value that updates automatically when fresh news or earnings arrive, so you can quickly see whether your fair value still sits above or below the current share price and decide how you want to act on that gap.

Do you think there's more to the story for Ulta Beauty? Head over to our Community to see what others are saying!

data.title: Assessing Borr Drilling (NYSE:BORR) Valuation After New Mexico And US Contract Wins
data.description:
Advertisement

What Borr Drilling’s New Contracts Mean for Investors

Borr Drilling (BORR) has drawn fresh attention after announcing new contract commitments for its Ran and Odin jack up rigs in Mexico and the United States, including additional extension options.

See our latest analysis for Borr Drilling.

These fresh contracts arrive as Borr Drilling’s share price sits at US$4.18, with a 90 day share price return of 58.33% and a 5 year total shareholder return of 143.80%. This suggests momentum has strengthened recently despite a weaker 3 year total shareholder return of 23.10%.

If rig contracts and utilization are on your radar, it can also be worth scanning other energy names through aerospace and defense stocks to see how different cash flow and risk profiles compare.

With new contracts lining up and the share price already up 58.33% over 90 days, the key question is whether Borr Drilling at US$4.18 still trades at a discount, or if markets are already pricing in future growth.

Most Popular Narrative: 1.6% Undervalued

With Borr Drilling closing at US$4.18 against a narrative fair value of US$4.25, the implied upside is modest but still framed as positive.

The valuation seems to price in that Borr Drilling's strong recent contract momentum, particularly in Mexico, the Middle East, and Southeast Asia, will translate into persistently high day rates and utilization. This view may underestimate the lingering risks from oversupply in the jack-up market and the increased volume of transitional or short-duration contracts, which could compress both future revenues and margins if the anticipated demand does not fully materialize.

Read the complete narrative.

Curious what sits behind that small valuation gap? The narrative leans on specific revenue, margin and earnings paths, all filtered through a 10.15% discount rate.

Result: Fair Value of $4.25 (UNDERVALUED)

Have a read of the narrative in full and understand what's behind the forecasts.

However, that small valuation gap could close quickly if jack-up oversupply pressures day rates, or if higher funding costs and leverage limit the value of new contracts.
